items.text: RuntimeKind enum with ClaudeRunner and Norn variants defined in types.rs
SessionConfig has an optional runtime field of type Option<RuntimeKind>
SessionHandle stores RuntimeKind and is queryable via the registry
AssistantError has NornBuildFailed and NornSessionFailed variants
AssistantService::start_session branches on RuntimeKind after token acquisition
spawn_norn_loop method builds AgentBuilder, creates event channels, spawns async task
Existing spawn_session_loop (Claude Runner path) is unchanged and continues to work
Both runtime paths register a SessionHandle with the same SessionRegistry
Both runtime paths produce broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent> for downstream consumers
run_norn_session_loop is an async function spawned via tokio::spawn (not spawn_blocking)
Norn session loop multiplexes AgentEvent broadcast, SessionCommand mpsc, idle timeout, and agent completion
SessionCommand::SendMessage delivers text via Norn InboundChannel
SessionCommand::Stop cancels via CancellationToken with graceful drain of remaining events
SessionCommand::Kill cancels via CancellationToken immediately
Idle timeout fires only after the agent has completed at least one turn
Session status transitions to Stopped when the Norn agent run completes
Completion watcher emits ServiceEvent::SessionCompleted or SessionFailed on the global EventBus
NornTranslateState defined in assistant/norn_translate.rs, parallel to TranslateState
ProviderEvent::TextDelta maps to AssistantEvent::TextDelta
ProviderEvent::ThinkingDelta maps to AssistantEvent::ThinkingDelta
ProviderEvent::ToolCallComplete maps to AssistantEvent::ToolUseStarted with complete input
ProviderEvent::ToolCallDelta maps to AssistantEvent::ToolInputDelta with resolved tool_call_id
ProviderEvent::ToolResult maps to AssistantEvent::ToolResult with is_error derived from output
ProviderEvent::TextComplete maps to AssistantEvent::AssistantMessage (replay-only snapshot)
ProviderEvent::ThinkingComplete maps to AssistantEvent::AssistantThinking (replay-only snapshot)
ProviderEvent::Done maps to AssistantEvent::Usage (is_turn_final: true) then TurnComplete
ProviderEvent::Error maps to AssistantEvent::Error with formatted message
NornTranslateState synthesizes SessionStarted from AgentBuilder config before first real event
Sub-agent events (agent_id != root) are wrapped in AssistantEvent::SubAgentEvent
Task/Agent tool calls emit SubAgentStarted, their results emit SubAgentCompleted
Per-turn state (tool IDs, sub-agents, block index map) is cleared on Done event
Norn sessions call AgentBuilder::session(store) with a JsonlSink-backed EventStore
Session JSONL files stored at {data_dir}/sessions/norn/{session_id}.jsonl
Session resume reads existing JSONL file and creates EventStore::with_sink_and_events
New sessions create EventStore::with_sink (empty events, append-only sink)
Session fork copies source JSONL to new path, then resumes from copy
PG persistence (persist_events) continues unchanged — subscribes to AssistantEvent broadcast
Redis streaming (stream_deltas_to_redis) continues unchanged
NornSessionStore type encapsulates server-side session file operations (create, resume, fork)
make_norn_step_runner calls .session() on AgentBuilder with workflow-scoped EventStore
Workflow session files stored at {data_dir}/sessions/workflow/{step_id}.jsonl
Norn sessions authenticate via OAuth (codex-login), independent of TokenPool (Tom direction 2026-05-28)
No pool token acquired for Norn sessions — provider uses AuthSource::OAuth
Claude Runner uses pool tokens, Norn uses independent OAuth — clean separation
build_wake_prompt unchanged — produces text prompt for both runtimes
Wake prompt delivered as AgentBuilder::prompt() for Norn sessions
Mid-session DMs delivered via SessionCommand::SendMessage
WakeupService unchanged — calls start_session which branches on RuntimeKind
Atomic claim via try_claim_for_wakeup works with Norn sessions
wake_configs table has a runtime column (text, nullable, default null = server default)
session_config_from_wake_config reads runtime field and sets SessionConfig.runtime
Server config has a default_runtime setting (ClaudeRunner during transition, Norn after)
CallerContext (from TA-001) carries member_id, session_id, workspace_id as Arc extension on ToolContext
Namespace tools call service-layer functions directly (in-process, no HTTP)
register_meridian_tools adds enabled namespace tools to AgentBuilder via .tool()
Profile filtering via allowed_tools/disallowed_tools gates namespace availability
Namespace tool implementations live in crates/meridian-tools/src/
Each namespace tool uses meridian_ prefix in the tool registry name
meridian_messaging tool implements 12 commands: send, inbox, read, search, mark_read, snooze, respond, retry, channel_send, channel_mentions, notify_check, notify_summary (aligned with TA-002)
meridian_member tool implements 7 commands: get, list, lookup, status_set, status_get, activity, set_profile (identity directory — hits MemberService)
meridian_workspace tool implements 4 commands: workspace_list, workspace_get, workspace_members, workspace_config (workspace environment — hits WorkspaceService)

meridian_source tool implements 25 commands: status, file_statuses, branches, log, blame, diff_file, diff_summary, tree_show, tree_add, tree_remove, tree_merge, tree_restack, tree_graft, worktree_list, worktree_create, worktree_remove, stage, unstage, stage_all, discard, commit, push, pull, fetch, remotes (aligned with TA-006)

meridian_branch tool implements 22 commands: branch_status, branch_submit, branch_land, branch_transition, branch_list, branch_show, branch_advance, stack_status, stack_submit, stack_sync, stack_land, stack_restack, pr_status, pr_create, pr_push, pr_sync, pr_approve, pr_request_changes, pr_comment, pr_merge, pr_close, pr_request_reviewers (aligned with TA-007)
meridian_workflow tool implements 6 workflow commands: workflow_list, workflow_show, workflow_run, workflow_status, workflow_history, workflow_cancel (aligned with TA-008)

meridian_workflow tool implements 8 scheduler commands: scheduler_list, scheduler_create, scheduler_get, scheduler_update, scheduler_delete, scheduler_enable, scheduler_disable, scheduler_run (redistributed from former admin tool, aligned with TA-008)

Former meridian_admin actions redistributed: workspace_list/get to meridian_workspace, channel_list/send to meridian_messaging, search to meridian_messaging, profile assignment to meridian_member (set_profile), scheduler to meridian_workflow
ws/assistant.rs compiles and passes all existing tests without modification after all NR-* briefs land
ws/session_replay compiles and passes all existing tests without modification after all NR-* briefs land
WS resubscribe (try_resubscribe) handles Norn broadcast sender drop identically to Claude Runner drop
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes clean
All existing assistant service tests pass without modification
Norn session loop tests verify complete event translation pipeline
Namespace tool tests verify all actions with mocked service layer
Session persistence tests verify create, resume, and fork flows
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
wake_trigger delivers DMs to active Norn sessions via SessionCommand::SendMessage instead of returning WakeSkipped
Mid-session DM formatted via format_agent_dm_envelope — identical envelope shape to wake prompt delivery, notification marked delivered after successful send
MessagingService exposes get_pending_notifications and count_pending_notifications wrapper methods delegating to NotificationStore
NotificationChecker struct holds Arc<MessagingService> and member_id, constructed in spawn_norn_loop, published as ToolContext extension
Post-tool-batch notification check injects pending notifications as ChannelMessage with DeliveryMode::Steer via InboundSender at the runner drain point
Injected notifications are marked as delivered so they are not re-injected on subsequent tool batches
norn-member.md profile exists with namespace tools (messaging, member, workspace, workflow), system_instructions for workspace member behavior, bash excluded
wake_config_session.rs idle_timeout_secs reads from wake_config or defaults to None instead of hardcoded Some(0)
Norn wake sessions with no explicit profile default to norn-member profile
Frontend session types include runtime_kind field, surfaced through useAssistantSession and useActiveAgentSessions hooks
No hardcoded 'claude' model fallback strings in AssistantPanel.tsx — runtime-aware fallback instead
Session toolbar displays a runtime badge (Norn or Claude Runner) in the ProfileRow with distinct visual treatment
